principle: As models get smarter, harnesses must get simpler — review deletion before addition

stages.name: setup_manual_enforce
stages.behavior: manual rule enforcement via pre-commit; gc run on demand

stages.name: autofix_proposal_loop
stages.behavior: scan mistakes.jsonl recurring (file × rule) patterns; emit proposals with known-fix templates; no auto-edit
stages.not_in_scope: automatic code rewriting — too risky for nexus deliberate violations

stages.name: weekly_gc_chain
stages.behavior: 7-day cooldown wrapper around gc.hexa --scan; safe to call from growth-tick or cron unconditionally
stages.pending: hooks-config.json registration requires user approval — not in this commit

stages.name: rule_usage_audit
stages.behavior: count each rule's hits across lint_log + gc_log within window; mark dormant (0 hits) as deletion candidates
stages.principle: before adding any new rule, run bitter-gate and prune dormant ones first

next: real-world verification of L1 (user local; sandbox blocked hexa exec)
register gc-weekly into growth-tick.hexa (user approval on hooks-config.json)
first bitter-gate --audit run after lint_log accumulates real traffic
consider L4: self-simplification — if bitter-gate keeps surfacing the same dormant rules, promote the prune itself to auto
forbidden: automatic source-code rewriting by autofix.hexa (violations in nexus are usually deliberate)
adding new rules without first running bitter-gate to prune dormant ones
